waves.name: Run Truth & Quality Gates
waves.goal: Make Cloudy produce trustworthy task outcomes instead of shallow success summaries.
gates: Every run produces a structured assessment artifact
Keel write-back includes evidence, checks, and risks instead of a one-line summary
Task close-out distinguishes review-ready from truly accepted
Acceptance drift between Keel and docs can be detected before closure

waves.name: Scheduler, Lanes, And Watchdog Discipline
waves.goal: Make cron-driven Cloudy runs safe, lane-aware, and self-monitoring.
gates: Watchdog loop can detect stuck or silent runs
Lane and product metadata are enforced enough to prevent common collisions
Cloudy can trigger the next ready task only when the queue state is trustworthy

waves.name: Operator Visibility
waves.goal: Make run history, dashboard views, and review state expose the real quality of a run.
gates: Run history exposes quality verdict, proof completeness, and risks
Dashboard surfaces review-ready vs accepted state clearly
Operators can see which runs are green, yellow, or red and why
